title: Leionema coxii
taxonomy: Leionema coxii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is a member of the genus Leionema, which is a group of small shrubs native to Australia. The species is further classified as Leionema coxii, with a specific epithet that distinguishes it from other species in the genus. Leionema coxii is a part of the larger taxonomic group called angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. In terms of its classification, Leionema coxii is often placed within the subclass Rosidae.
uses: Leionema coxii is a plant with traditional uses in Australian Aboriginal medicine. However, there is limited research on its specific medicinal properties.
